title: I with diaeresis (Cyrillic)
summary: I with diaeresis (Ӥ ӥ; italics: Ӥ ӥ) is a letter of the Cyrillic script. It is used in the Udmurt language, where it represents the close front unrounded vowel /i/, and is used only after the non-palatalized dentals /d/, /z/, /l/, /n/, /s/ and /t/; the Cyrillic letter I (Ии) is used otherwise.
text:
I with diaeresis (Ӥ ӥ; italics: Ӥ ӥ) is a letter of the Cyrillic script. It is used in the Udmurt language, where it represents the close front unrounded vowel /i/, and is used only after the non-palatalized dentals /d/, /z/, /l/, /n/, /s/ and /t/; the Cyrillic letter I (Ии) is used otherwise.

Computing codes
U+04E4 Ӥ CYRILLIC CAPITAL LETTER I WITH DIAERESIS
U+04E5 ӥ CYRILLIC SMALL LETTER I WITH DIAERESIS
